summary: Russian air defenses reported downing 245 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian territory and the Black Sea. Ukrainian drones struck fuel depots of Ukrnafta in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, as well as Naftogaz assets in Poltava. Russian UAV attacks hit civilian targets in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia beach, Konotop cinema and caused a blackout in Sevastopol.
key_points: Russia's air defence shot down 245 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Ukrainian drones attacked Ukrnafta fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Russian drones struck a fuel station on the outskirts of Sumy, injuring three civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
A Russian drone attack on a beach in Zaporizhzhia injured four people, including three children (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
Drone strikes hit the Konotop cinema in Sumy region, causing multiple civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
A night‑time drone raid on Sevastopol caused a blackout and a fire at the main substation (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported near Zaporizhzhia heading toward Novomykolaivka, and near Dnipropetrovsk heading toward Shakhtarske and Pavlohrad (Source: @kpszsu/66234).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east front, raising threat of air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66223).

sections.executive_summary: Russia’s air defence claimed to have downed 245 Ukrainian UAVs over its territory and the Black Sea. Ukrainian drones have successfully hit fuel infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, disrupting Ukrnafta operations. Russian drone strikes continue to target civilian sites in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Sevastopol, causing injuries and power outages.

points: Ukrainian UAVs attacked Ukrnafta fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, forcing the stations to stop operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
A UAV was observed heading from the south‑east toward Zaporizhzhia city, targeting the western outskirts (Source: @kpszsu/66231).
Ukrainian drones were reported over the north‑west of Kharkiv region, striking railway trains carrying military supplies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31135).
Two large drones crashed on the Turkish Black Sea coast; local authorities suspect they were Ukrainian kamikaze drones (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31128).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted on the north‑east front, with potential for guiding strike weapons (Source: @kpszsu/66223).
Ukrainian drone sightings included a reactive UAV near Aviatorskyi in Dnipropetrovsk, likely for reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/98053).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from the south‑east toward the western outskirts of Kherson (Source: @kpszsu/66232).
Russian air defence reported shooting down 245 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east front, indicating a threat of air strikes on front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/66223).

Ukrainian UAVs were reported moving from the south‑east toward Zaporizhzhia city, indicating pressure on eastern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/66231).

key_developments: Russia’s air defence reported shooting down 245 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions and the Black Sea, indicating high UAV activity (Source: @tass_agency/382259).
Ukrainian drones struck Ukrnafta fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, halting fuel operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Russian drones attacked a Sumy fuel station, injuring three civilians, showing continued UAV use against civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82145).
A Russian drone strike on Zaporizhzhia beach injured four people, including three children, highlighting civilian risk (Source: @UkraineNow/82150).
Drone attacks on the Konotop cinema in Sumy region caused multiple injuries, demonstrating targeting of public venues (Source: @UkraineNow/82146).
Night‑time drone raids on Sevastopol caused a blackout and a fire at the main substation, affecting critical infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82140).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward Novomykolaivka in Zaporizhzhia and Shakhtarske in Dnipropetrovsk, indicating ongoing offensive UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/66234).
sections.threat_assessment: UAV activity remains intense on both sides, with Russia claiming high interception rates while Ukraine continues to target fuel logistics and transport hubs. Russian drone strikes on civilian sites in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia and Sevastopol raise the risk of collateral damage and undermine public morale. The presence of tactical aviation on the north‑east front adds an air‑to‑ground threat to Ukrainian forward positions, while Ukrainian UAVs maintain pressure on Russian supply lines and infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are reinforcing air defence and counter‑UAV measures, especially around fuel depots and transport corridors. Russian officials emphasize the success of air defence but continue to employ drones against civilian targets, suggesting a strategy of disruption. Local administrations in affected regions have reported injuries and infrastructure damage, prompting emergency response and civilian alerts.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows repeated corridors from the south‑east toward Zaporizhzhia and from the north toward Sumy, indicating preferred strike routes. The high number of downed UAVs reported by Russia may reflect increased electronic warfare or improved air defence capabilities. Continued Russian tactical aviation activity on the north‑east front suggests preparation for possible air strikes, requiring heightened alert for Ukrainian ground forces.
